title: Prosperity Club — Financial education for ordinary people
description: Deborah Armstrong on insurance, investing, social class, and the strategies the wealthy use to keep more of what they earn. Free articles + a 5-week Investing Masterclass.

footer.colophon: © 2026 Prosperity Club, Deborah Armstrong. Content for educational purposes only. Not financial, legal, or investment advice.

sections.title: Build wealth on terms you can actually use.
sections.lede: Most financial advice assumes you already have money. Prosperity Club walks you through the parts no one teaches: how insurance and tax strategy compound over decades, how the wealthy actually keep what they earn, and how ordinary people can apply the same tools at their own scale.

sections.text: Three deep-dives on the topics most readers come back to. Each is free, sourced, and written for working and middle-class readers in the US and Canada.
Why your job pays you less than you're worth.

The minimum wage today is worth 40% less than it was in 1968. A household earning $75,000 can only afford 21% of home listings. The wage-housing gap has compounded into a generational shift the headlines mostly miss.
What the wealthy do that you can do too.

Buy, Borrow, Die. 1031 exchanges. Roth IRA + HSA stacking. None of these are loopholes — they are statutes written into the tax code, often for over a century. The article walks through each in plain language and where ordinary investors can apply the same principles.
Before maximizing returns, protect what you have. Disability, life, health, home/renters, auto — covered honestly, including which products are oversold and which are quietly load-bearing.

sections.body: The rich don't avoid taxes by cheating. They avoid taxes by understanding the rules better than everyone else. The rules are public. The tax code is published.

sections.text: Five weeks. Member-only ($5/month). Covers strategy, structure, and keeping what you build — not how to pick stocks. For readers who want to think about money the way people with money think about it.

items.hint: Why insurance is the foundation; how to size term life + disability.

items.value: The tax code is the strategy
items.hint: Roth IRA + HSA stacking; 1031 exchange basics; what Buy, Borrow, Die actually means.
Which accounts hold what, and why most retail advice gets this backwards.
Index funds vs. picks; rebalancing rules; what NOT to trade.
Estate planning baseline; beneficiary hygiene; the documents everyone should already have.

sections.lede: The blog is fully free. The Masterclass and the Risk Management consultation are for members. Cancel any time.
